textPlain: I wish all projects like these had some sort of documentation as to how they worked. All I can see is that the `-B` flag tells GCC to look in the ased directory for internal binaries (cc1plus and ld), but anything other than that would require more than the few minutes I have to look into.It's also really interesting to see the audience this is reaching. The issues on the GitHub repository are quite telling.
great video and source but needs a try-catch block
Me too. He is so creative. I came across this repo and realized he also made a video about it.

As far as I can tell, it looks like a wrapper for cc1plus (internal GCC tool) and ld that do a custom #define of some input data, compile, extract some data from the binary, and do... something with it. Makes it look like C++ is an interpreted language.
